body:
the `buffer_*_parameters` are not returning concrete types because the `KernelParameters` are constructed by runtime-variables. This PR fixes the instability by computing the "local direction" from topologies which are compile-time constants and in turn allows the `buffer_*_parameters` functions to return fully concrete `KernelParameters`.

This is an instability which is hit only in asynchronous-distributed computations.
title: Remove type-instability in `buffer_*_parameters`

body:
The `multi_region` group takes ~1h44m on CPU, dominated by the cubed sphere simulation testset (~61 of ~96 min of test time), of which the two immersed-grid runs alone are ~40 min.

This splits that testset out of `test_multi_region_cubed_sphere.jl` into two new test groups that run as separate parallel Buildkite jobs:

- `multi_region_simulation` — underlying-grid runs (~21 min)
- `multi_region_simulation_immersed` — immersed-grid runs (~40 min)

`multi_region` keeps the unit, advection-diffusion, and cubed-sphere geometry/halo tests (~36 min). The shared per-grid test body lives in `test_multi_region_cubed_sphere_simulation_utils.jl`, included by both drivers. No test content changed; `TEST_GROUP=all` still runs everything.

Net effect: the critical path for these tests drops from ~1h44m to roughly the slowest of the three jobs (~40 min + setup), provided the agent queue has spare capacity.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

labels.description: Tests get priority in case of emergency evacuation
labels.name: testing 🧪

title: Speed up multi_region cubed sphere and zstar tests

body: `PerturbationAdvection` still had a face-field indexing for the left boundary conditions even when used with center fields. Somehow this didn't create any problems in the validation scripts and tests. I'm trying to come up with a MRE that catches this difference.

labels.description: Even a perfect program still has bugs
labels.name: bug 🐞

title: Bugfix in left center-field boundary conditions with `PerturbationAdvection`
